conflicts.description: Vendor pricing control contradiction: TR-008 and TR-022/US-016 imply vendors manage product information (which could include pricing), but TR-020 explicitly prohibits vendors from setting prices, while TR-007/TR-059/US-014 assign pricing exclusively to central admin.

version_a.text: The system shall allow vendors to create, update, and manage their product listings.

version_b.text: Vendors cannot set product prices; only RetailCorp central admin controls pricing.

conflicts.description: SAP sync frequency contradiction: TR-026 requires inventory levels to sync with SAP ERP in real time, but TR-027 requires product information to sync from SAP via batch process once daily. US-015 further reinforces real-time sync. If product information includes stock/inventory data, these requirements directly conflict.

version_a.text: Inventory levels must sync with SAP ERP in real time.

version_b.text: Product information must sync from SAP via batch process once daily.

conflicts.description: GDPR compliance vs. India-only data storage contradiction: TR-043 requires GDPR compliance for NRI customer data handling, which may require data portability, right to erasure, and potentially data residency in the EU or accessible cross-border, while TR-045 mandates all customer data must be stored on servers physically located within India, which may conflict with GDPR obligations for EU-resident NRI customers.

version_a.text: Platform must comply with GDPR regulations for NRI customer data handling.

version_b.text: All customer data must be stored on servers physically located within India.

conflicts.description: Vendor product listing management vs. central admin exclusive catalog control: TR-022 and US-016 allow vendors to manage product information and descriptions, while TR-058 and US-013 state central admin must be able to manage product listings across the entire catalog, creating ambiguity and potential conflict over who has authority to create/update product listings.

version_a.text: Vendors must be able to manage product information and descriptions via the portal.

version_b.text: Central admin must be able to manage product listings across the entire catalog.

conflicts.description: ZestMoney partnership assumption vs. go-live timeline constraint: TR-018 assumes the ZestMoney partnership agreement will be finalized before platform go-live, but no corresponding functional requirement for ZestMoney integration exists, and TR-009 constrains go-live to 6 months. If ZestMoney is required for EMI functionality alongside TR-015 (Bajaj Finance), the dependency on an unfinalized agreement conflicts with the fixed go-live deadline.

version_a.text: ZestMoney partnership agreement will be finalized before platform go-live.

version_b.text: Platform must go live within 6 months maximum from project start.

gaps.message: 'Business Context and Background' has limited coverage.
'Project Objectives and Success Criteria' has limited coverage.
'User Journeys and Use Cases' has limited coverage.
